ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for steamed glutinous rice cake stuffed with red bean paste; closest parent is glutinous rice cake food product (FOODON_03315671).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Rice cake) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for glutinous rice cake with red bean filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific confection.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 2 pieces of steamed glutinous rice cake, each approximately 50 g, totalling approximately 100 g.

nutrient_source.title: Red bean glutinous rice cake (steamed) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimate based on glutinous rice flour and sweetened red bean paste components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for steamed glutinous rice cake stuffed with red bean paste exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data: glutinous rice flour (FDC ID 169717) and sweetened red bean paste (adzuki bean paste, FDC ID 174289), combined in approximate proportions of 55% glutinous rice dough and 45% red bean paste filling, consistent with standard Din Tai Fung-style preparation. Energy calculated using Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of glutinous rice and red bean paste products — analogous estimate based on glutinous rice (GI ~98) and sweetened red bean paste (GI ~43) in approximate 55:45 ratio; weighted mean GI ~72 adjusted downward to ~65 for steaming and fibre contribution from red bean
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Glutinous rice GI is reported as approximately 98 (University of Sydney GI Database, Kaye Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002;76:5–56). Sweetened red bean paste (adzuki bean) GI is approximately 43 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted mean for 55% glutinous rice dough and 45% red bean paste filling yields approximately 72; adjusted to 65 to account for steaming gelatinisation effects and dietary fibre from red beans moderating glycaemic response. GL per 100 g serving = (65 × 54.5) / 100 = 35.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~28 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 28 mg sodium per 100 g, steamed glutinous rice cake with red bean paste is a low-sodium dessert and poses minimal direct concern for blood pressure management when consumed in standard 2-piece portions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index (~65) and high carbohydrate load (~54.5 g per 100 g) with significant added sugar (~18 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high carbohydrate content and medium-to-high glycaemic index of this glutinous rice-based dessert can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one piece and pair with a protein or fat source to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (~0.2 g per 100 g) and zero cholesterol; moderate sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The negligible saturated fat and cholesterol content make this dessert relatively benign from a lipid perspective for cardiovascular disease patients, though the moderate sugar content warrants portion control to avoid contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Glutinous Rice Cakes Stuffed with Mashed Red Bean (松糕, Sōng Gāo) are a classic Chinese confection made from glutinous rice flour dough that is steamed until soft and slightly chewy, then filled with a smooth, sweetened red bean paste (dòushā) and whole or partially mashed red beans. The texture is characteristically soft, pillowy, and mildly sticky, with a delicate sweetness from the red bean filling contrasting with the neutral, slightly elastic rice cake exterior. This dessert is widely associated with Taiwanese and mainland Chinese dim sum culture and is a signature item at Din Tai Fung restaurants in Singapore and internationally. Nutritionally, the dish is predominantly carbohydrate-dense (~54.5 g per 100 g), with moderate dietary fibre (~3.2 g per 100 g) contributed by the red bean filling, low fat (~0.8 g per 100 g), and modest protein (~4.8 g per 100 g). The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 65 (medium-high), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The dish is naturally dairy-free, egg-free, and suitable for vegetarian and vegan diets. Clinically, the high carbohydrate and sugar content (~18 g sugar per 100 g) requires attention for Type 2 Diabetes management, while the low sodium and saturated fat profile make it relatively acceptable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients in small portions.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific product was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for glutinous rice flour and sweetened red bean paste in standard proportions. GI is an analogous estimate derived from University of Sydney GI Database values for glutinous rice and adzuki bean paste. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the proportions are consistent with standard preparation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for glutinous rice flour (FDC ID 169717) and sweetened red bean paste (FDC ID 174289) in a 55:45 ratio. Direct laboratory analysis of this specific product would improve confidence to high. 2. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this exact product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the component sources used. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for these plant-based components. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for these components. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for these components. 7. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as this is a plant-based steamed product with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero by formulation. 8. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based product containing no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero by formulation. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 as glutinous rice flour and red bean paste contain negligible vitamin C; confirmed trace/zero per USDA SR Legacy. 10. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based product with no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero. 11. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Grains and staples' (input) to 'Desserts' as this is a sweetened confection served as dessert, consistent with its culinary context at Din Tai Fung.
